claim: Personal AI assistants need three core elements: ears (real-time STT), brain (LLM), and voice (TTS); always-on listening is feasible with local models
actionable: Implement always-on STT using RealtimeSTT library; trigger brain activation only on keyword; avoid transcribing unless needed
source_title: Deepseek AI Assistant: ALWAYS ON Python AI Agent for Engineers that SHIP

claim: Scratch Pad pattern: active memory that logs agent outputs and accepts manual updates; enables agent to learn from its own execution and user edits
actionable: Maintain a mutable scratchpad file that persists agent decisions; allow users to edit it; pass full context to agent on next invocation
source_title: Deepseek AI Assistant: ALWAYS ON Python AI Agent for Engineers that SHIP

claim: RealtimeSTT: tiny model transcribes locally in <1.3 seconds with tradeoff in accuracy; large-v3 takes ~18 seconds but is perfect for high-stakes tasks
actionable: Use tiny model for responsive UX; large model for critical tasks; let users toggle model size in config
source_title: Deepseek AI Assistant: ALWAYS ON Python AI Agent for Engineers that SHIP

claim: DeepSeek V3 is cost-effective and capable enough for always-on agents; 100% local setup possible but sacrifices speed (uses AMA or similar local LLM)
actionable: Use DeepSeek V3 via API for production; consider local Llama for privacy-sensitive deployments accepting 20-30% accuracy loss
source_title: Deepseek AI Assistant: ALWAYS ON Python AI Agent for Engineers that SHIP

claim: Typer CLI agent wraps command execution; pass soul.md (identity), agents.md (tools), and scratchpad (context); agent decides which commands to run
actionable: Structure CLI commands in Typer; embed agent instructions in markdown files; use natural language routing rather than explicit CLI args
